briefdescription.para: Interrupt handler type for unsupported data type.
Interrupt handler type for pixel data underrun.
Interrupt handler type for other errors.
Interrupt handler type for command queue FIFO full.
Number of packets for long packet data in command mode.
DSI Tx controller Disable.
DSI Tx controller Enable.
DSI Controller Enable/Disable Selection.
Video mode.
Command mode.
This typedef contains the DSI mode selection.
Command mode short packet.
Command mode long packet.
This typedef contains the type of Command mode Packet.
Non-burst mode with sync pulses.
Non-burst mode with sync event.
Burst mode.
Number of supported video modes.
Video Timing Mode by default Non-burst mode with Sync Events.
Callback type for all interrupts defined.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the event. For current core version, this parameter is "OR" of 0 or more XDSI_ISR_*_MASK constants defined in xdsi_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set bit in a register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
is the part of the bitname before _MASK

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset bit in a register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
is the part of the bitname before _MASK

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegisterOffset, u32 BitMask, u32 BitShift)
memberdef.name: XDsi_GetBitField

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the value of bitfield from register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
is the part of the bitname before _MASK
is the part of the bitname before _SHIFT

simplesect.para: Bit Field Value in u32 format

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegisterOffset, u32 BitMask, u32 BitShift, u32 Value)
memberdef.name: XDsi_SetBitField

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the value of bitfield from register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
is the part of the bitname before _MASK
is the part of the bitname before _SHIFT
is to be set. Passed in u32 format.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to start the soft reset process by setting the soft reset bit in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI2 instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if a controller is ready or still in reset stage.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to stop the soft reset process by resetting the soft reset bit in the Core Configuration Register.This is done usually after Reset in Progress is 0.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to stop the packet processing by resetting Enable core bit in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to start the packet processing by setting Enable core bit in the Core Configuration Register.This is done after the configuration of active lanes, interrupt masks, etc.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset the command fifo by setting Command FIFO Reset bit in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset the command fifo by clearing Command FIFO Reset in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset the command fifo by setting Data FIFO Reset bit in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset the command fifo by clearing Data FIFO Reset bit in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if the Core is enabled by checking the core enable bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: SET or RESET in u32 format

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if the Command/video mode is enabled by checking Command Mode bit in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: SET (for command mode) or RESET(for video mode) in u32 format

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set Command Mode bit in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to clear Command Mode bit in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to enable the global interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This is used after setting the interrupts mask before enabling the core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to disable the global interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This is done after disabling the core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set interrupt mask to enable interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This is done before enabling the core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.
Interrupts to be enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to find out which interrupts are registered for.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to disable interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.
Interrupts to be disabled.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to find out which events have triggered the interrupt source, presently DSI supporting Undefined data type and pixel under flow error, the ISR register bits will set when respective interrupt triggers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to acknowledge/clear the events.

detaileddescription.para: Once the interrupt occur the same value need to write in ISR register

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.
is Bit Mask for ack of interrupts

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get pixel format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: 0x0E – Packed RGB565 0x1E- packed RGB666 0x2E – Loosely packed RGB666 0x3E- Packed RGB888 0x0B- Compressed Pixel Stream

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get Command queue Vacancy.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Number of command queue entries can be safely written to Command queue FIFO, before it goes full.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get readiness status to short packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: SET(ready to send short packet) or RESET(not ready to send short packet) in u32 format

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get readiness status to Long packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: SET(ready to send short packet) or RESET(not ready to send short packet) in u32 format

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get Data FIFO full status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: SET(FIFO is full) or RESET(FIFO is not full) in u32 format

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get Data FIFO Empty status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: SET(FIFO is Empty) or RESET(FIFO is not Empty) in u32 format

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get wait status for data to process long command.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: SET(long command is in progress) or RESET(Long command is processed) in u32 format

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get in progress status for Short/Long Commands.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: SET(Long command) or RESET(short command) in u32 format

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get under process status for Short/Long Commands.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the DSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: SET(Long command) or RESET(short command) in u32 format

briefdescription.para: Look up the hardware configuration for a device instance.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device to lookup for

simplesect.para: The reference to the configuration record in the configuration table (in xdsi_g.c) corresponding to the Device ID or if not found, a NULL pointer is returned.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the XDsi instance provided by the caller based on the given Config structure.

detaileddescription.para: Initializes the DSI controller instance with hardware configuration information and sets up default callback handlers.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on.
is the device configuration structure containing information about a specific DSI controller (base address, lanes, FIFO size, etc.)
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

simplesect.para: All callback handlers are initialized to stub functions that will assert on invocation. Application must set actual callback handlers using XDsi_SetCallback() if interrupt handling is required.

briefdescription.para: Enable or disable the DSI controller core.

detaileddescription.para: Activates or deactivates the DSI controller IP core. When enabling (Flag=XDSI_ENABLE), the global interrupt output is set and the core is enabled for packet processing. When disabling (Flag=XDSI_DISABLE), the global interrupt is reset and the core is disabled.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on. The instance must have been initialized via XDsi_CfgInitialize().

indicates the desired action:
XDSI_ENABLE: Enable global interrupt and activate DSI core
XDSI_DISABLE: Reset global interrupt and disable DSI core

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if enable/disable operation was successful.
XST_INVALID_PARAM if an invalid Flag value is provided.

simplesect.para: Must be called after XDsi_CfgInitialize() and XDsi_DefaultConfigure(). The core must be configured with video mode or command mode settings before activation.

briefdescription.para: Perform a soft reset of the DSI controller.

detaileddescription.para: Resets the DSI controller including interrupt status registers, command queue FIFO, and state machine. The controller gracefully waits for current operations to complete before resetting. After reset is released, controller starts from VSS (Vertical Sync Start) packet (new video frame).

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on.

briefdescription.para: Configure default DSI protocol settings.

detaileddescription.para: Configures the DSI controller protocol register with video mode, blank packet mode, blank packet type, and End-of-Transmission (EoTP) packet settings based on the instance parameters. This function sets up the Protocol Control Register (PCR) with the instance VideoMode, BlankPacketType, BLLPMode, and EoTp configuration values.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on. The instance must have been initialized via XDsi_CfgInitialize().

simplesect.para: This function assumes the instance is already initialized. The instance member variables (VideoMode, BlankPacketType, BLLPMode, EoTp) must be set before calling this function or it will use default/uninitialized values.

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: This test checks if the LaneCount present in register matches the one from the generated file.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDsi instance.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the read value was not equal to GUI parameter

briefdescription.para: Send a DSI short packet to the controller.

detaileddescription.para: Sends a DSI short packet (up to 2 data bytes) to the DSI controller via the Generic Short Packet Register. The function packs the Virtual Channel ID, Data Type, and two data bytes into the command register format and writes to the XDSI_COMMAND_OFFSET register. Short packets are typically used for commands and status updates.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on. The instance must have been initialized and activated.

is a pointer to the XDsi_ShortPacket structure containing:
VcId: Virtual channel identifier (0-3)
DataType: DSI data type code for the packet
Data0: First data byte
Data1: Second data byte

simplesect.para: This function does not wait for transmission to complete. The caller must check controller status if synchronization is needed. Only valid for sending up to 2 bytes of data; use XDsi_SendLongPacket() for longer payloads.

briefdescription.para: Send a DSI command mode packet (short or long).

detaileddescription.para: Sends a DSI packet in command mode. This function supports both short packets (up to 2 bytes) and long packets (up to 255 bytes). It waits for the controller to be ready for the appropriate packet type before transmission. For short packets, waits on XDSI_GetReadyForShortPkt(); for long packets, waits on XDSI_GetReadyForLongPkt().

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on. The instance must have been initialized, activated, and switched to command mode via XDsi_SetMode(XDSI_COMMAND_MODE).

is a pointer to the XDsiTx_CmdModePkt structure specifying:
CmdPkt: Packet type (XDSI_CMD_MODE_SHORT_PKT or XDSI_CMD_MODE_LONG_PKT)
SpktData: Short packet header information
LongPktData: Payload for long packets

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the DSI packet was successfully sent.
XST_FAILURE if DSI is not enabled or not in command mode.

simplesect.para: This function blocks waiting for the controller to be ready. Ensure controller is in command mode before calling. The appropriate packet type must be selected in CmdPktData->CmdPkt.

briefdescription.para: Switch DSI controller between video and command modes.

detaileddescription.para: Configures the DSI controller to operate in either video mode or command mode. In video mode, the controller processes continuous video frame data streams with synchronization packets. In command mode, the controller accepts individual short and long packets sent by the application. Transitions between modes include appropriate waits for pending operations to complete.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on. The instance must have been initialized via XDsi_CfgInitialize().

is the desired DSI mode:
XDSI_VIDEO_MODE: Configure for continuous video stream processing
XDSI_COMMAND_MODE: Configure for packet-based command transmission

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if mode switch was successful.
XST_INVALID_PARAM if the requested mode is invalid or an invalid transition is attempted.

simplesect.para: Certain mode transitions are not allowed by the hardware. For example, cannot switch from video mode to command mode directly while in progress. The function waits for XDsi_GetInProgress() to clear before switching.

briefdescription.para: Read current DSI controller configuration parameters.

detaileddescription.para: Reads the current configuration settings from the DSI controller hardware registers and the driver instance. This includes the protocol control settings (video mode, blank packet type, BLLP mode, EoTP), timing parameters (HSA, VACT, blanking intervals, etc.), and line/BLLP timing. All settings are populated into the provided ConfigInfo structure.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on. The instance must have been initialized via XDsi_CfgInitialize().

is a pointer to the XDsi_ConfigParameters structure to be filled with current configuration. The structure will contain:
Config: GUI configuration settings
VideoMode: Current video transmission mode
BlankPacketType: Blanking packet type for BLLP
BLLPMode: Blank packet mode selection
EoTp: End-of-Transmission packet enable status
Timing: All horizontal/vertical timing parameters
LineTime, BLLPTime: Total line and BLLP timing values

simplesect.para: This function performs multiple register reads. The caller must ensure the controller is in a stable state before reading. Timing parameters reflect current register values which may differ from hard-coded configuration if modified at runtime.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDsi *InstancePtr, XDsi_VideoMode VideoMode, XVidC_VideoMode Resolution, u16 BurstPacketSize)
memberdef.name: XDsi_SetVideoInterfaceTiming

briefdescription.para: Configure video interface timing based on standard resolution.

detaileddescription.para: Sets the DSI controller video interface timing parameters based on a specified video mode and standard resolution. Looks up timing parameters from the video common library (XVidC_GetVideoModeData), including horizontal and vertical synchronization, active regions, and blanking intervals. The burst packet size is set for burst mode operations. This function is typically called for standard video resolutions (e.g., 1920x1080@60Hz).

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on. The instance must have been initialized via XDsi_CfgInitialize().

specifies the DSI video transmission mode:
XDSI_VM_NON_BURST_SYNC_PULSES: Non-burst with sync pulses
XDSI_VM_NON_BURST_SYNC_EVENT: Non-burst with sync events
XDSI_VM_BURST_MODE: Burst mode transmission
specifies the video resolution to configure (e.g., XVIDC_VM_1920x1080_60_P for 1920x1080@60Hz progressive).
specifies the packet size for burst mode blanking.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if video interface timing was successfully configured.
XST_FAILURE if the requested resolution is not supported or timing information cannot be retrieved.

simplesect.para: This function looks up timing data from the video common library. The requested resolution must be supported by the library. For custom resolutions not in the library, use XDsi_SetCustomVideoInterfaceTiming().

briefdescription.para: Configure video interface timing with custom parameters.

detaileddescription.para: Sets the DSI controller video interface timing parameters using user-provided custom timing values. Unlike XDsi_SetVideoInterfaceTiming() which uses standard resolutions from the video library, this function allows arbitrary custom timing configurations. Horizontal and vertical synchronization, active region, and blanking parameters are all configurable. Burst packet size is only applied in burst mode.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on. The instance must have been initialized via XDsi_CfgInitialize().

specifies the DSI video transmission mode:
XDSI_VM_NON_BURST_SYNC_PULSES: Non-burst with sync pulses
XDSI_VM_NON_BURST_SYNC_EVENT: Non-burst with sync events
XDSI_VM_BURST_MODE: Burst mode (burst packet size applies)

is a pointer to the XDsi_VideoTiming structure containing custom timing parameters:
HActive: Active horizontal pixels/bytes per line
HFrontPorch, HSyncWidth, HBackPorch: Horizontal blanking intervals
VActive: Active vertical lines
VFrontPorch, VSyncWidth, VBackPorch: Vertical blanking intervals
BLLPBurst: Burst packet size (only used in burst mode)

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if video interface timing was successfully configured.

simplesect.para: This function allows maximum flexibility but does not validate timing parameters for spec compliance. Burst packet size is only written in burst mode; non-burst modes override it to 0. Ensure custom timing values comply with DSI and display controller specifications.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Invoked by this driver when: ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------- XDSI_HANDLER_UNSUPPORT_DATATYPE Un support data type detected XDSI_HANDLER_PIXELDATA_UNDERRUN Byte stream FIFO starves for Pixel during HACT transmission XDSI_HANDLER_OTHERERROR Any other type of interrupt has occurred like Stream Line Buffer Full, Incorrect Lanes, etc XDSI_HANDLER_CMDQ_FIFOFULL Command queue FIFO full

parameterdescription.para: is the XDsi instance to operate on
is the type of call back to be registered.
is the pointer to a call back function which is called when a particular event occurs.
is a void pointer to data to be referenced to by the CallbackFunc

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.
